sentences.text: But they're both in the waiting room, so I'll let them in, I figure we could just shoot the shit, talk about lunch next week, see how things are going.
I don't have anything, like, crazy, you know, updates-wise, but we'll go from there.
Yeah, but they had a reputation provider, too, right?
No, they were do- right now, they're doing it manually.
I'll let him in, and I'll look at this recap while we're letting him in and chatting with him.
Hey, Patrick, how was your Labor Day?
My cousin was in town, so, like, we were doing all the touristy stuff, and it was just… it was a lot.
You got to start doing Boston tourist stuff?
We accidentally, like, went to the festival in the north end, and it was.
Absolutely… we didn't know it was happening.
But we got Ernesto, so I was like, you know what?
Yeah, that's a dangerous thing to stumble into.
Yeah, all of a sudden, it was crazy, and our friends lived on the block of the festival.
I'm like, how have you been able to leave your apartment today?
Francis, it was some St, like, food festival, took over, like, 3 blocks, a lot of food vendors, smelled great, but just crazy busy business.
Yeah, they do, like, 3 or 4 of these feasts, they're called feasts a year, and, like, the whole north end just kind of shuts them down, and there's a whole… There's a whole thing.
Is that fun, or is that crazy, like, touristy, hectic?
It's, yeah, it's a little bit of both.
It's bad for everyone moving, because yesterday was, like, the big moving day in Boston, and it's like, how do you move in the North End?
Yeah, that… yeah, if I would have been pissed if I was, like, a…
a parent trying to move my kid in over there, but… yeah, we stayed away from Boston yesterday.
I mentioned to my wife, I was like, let's take the kids in, like, and she's like, no, it's moving day.
What is moving… why does Boston have a moving day?
There's so many colleges, there's so many colleges, like, and all the kids move in Labor Day weekend.
Labor Day, so I live in Madison, Wisconsin, so… all the college kids moved in, like, late August.
September 1, across Boston, every single lease starts.
Like, Patrick, I'm on a September 1 lease, we could not find a single other start date, like.
Wait, so classes start probably today, and the move-in day was yesterday?
Madison, we building a buffer out here, we got a whole week.
I live in the Midwest, so forgive me.
a huge, like, pizza place, biggest slice in Boston.
John, you're gonna find this funny.
I accidentally bought two slices of pizza.
Patrick, it was, like, an entire individual-sized pizza.
It's, like, the equivalent of four normal slices.
But I was, like, then carrying a box of pizza around because I was like, I can't eat 4 normal slices of pizza.
I'm gonna say, I think I could eat 4 normal slices of pizza.
No, for context, like, it looks cut in the middle, that's one slice.
They just cut it in half for you, so I…
I got two of those, and I was just like…
- it's like a quarter of a large pizza.
And would Elise go up there and look down on it?
Because she's got her New Haven pizza, you know?
Yeah, would I approve of it?
I hope so, it was really damn good.
I love pizza, so, you know…
I mean, better than Regina's pizza, which is controversial to some.
John, have you ever been to New Haven for pizza?
Yes, but I don't remember it.
I'll tell you, William, you're shaking.
But I've heard it's really good.
Yeah, I've been there for, yeah, I was there… I've been there a bunch, but not for pizza every time.
I got pizza before a concert, like, 20 years ago, so I don't remember much of it.
Were you at, like, Toad's Place or something?
Yeah, I went to, like, Toad's place for, like, I think it was a Tool, like, Tool, which is not my music.
I'm not… I'm like a hippie guy.
my girlfriend at the time, we saw a tool in a small club, which was absolute insanity.

There was buckets filled with, like, contraband that had been, like… I'm used to, like, concerts where they're, like, taking, like, bongs and stuff and putting it in, like, you know, not, like, not, like, brass knuckles and other stuff.
You were in New Haven, so… I don't call Pistol Wave in New Haven for nothing, John.
The downside, like, there's, like, the cool artsy area, and then it gets completely sketched.
Like, if you take a wrong turn in New Haven, you're in trouble.
New Haven, New Haven is a.

Yeah, it's so weird, because I, like, I grew up in New Haven, and, like, even just, like, as a young college girl, like, even late high school, we used to hang out in the downtown area, like, walk around, and it was always, like, there was always, like, what you're describing, but not as bad as it is now.

even, like, where I lived in New Haven, like, you could be in, like, one really, really nice area, which I guess this is anywhere, but it's, like, really extreme in New Haven, where you could be, like.
On a really nice street with beautiful homes, and just, like, you know… and then you take, like, one wrong turn.
You can be in, like, in the Hollywood Hills, and then, like, a mile later, you're in the ghetto.
Alright, well, we got a lot done here.
Keep going, let's do it, Patrick, I like this.
Are you a Patriots guy or no?
Oh yeah, yeah, I'm a Patriots guy.
How do we feel about… how do we feel about it?
It's a great time to be a fan, man.
How do we feel about Bill Belichick getting crushed at his…
Okay, so are Boston fans thinking it's funny, or how do we feel about this in Boston?
I think they think it's funny.
I think everyone's kind of done with the Belichick thing.
I think people still respect what he did, but it's… yeah.
He's running around like a 20-year-old girl or something like that, it's all in that way.
The way I see it is, like, like, you know, being from the area, it's like, you're pumped to see, like, a Brady statue.
And it's like, you almost feel like they'd have to do a Belichick statue, but that just almost feels a little, like, weirder.
I don't know, John, what do you think?
Yeah, no, the Brady statue, I already took my kids down to that, like, they were saying, yeah, that's good.
It's a little disproportionate, it doesn't exactly look like him.
Unless maybe in real life it does, and in the picture it just didn't, but we were like, we were like, what… I'm a Giants fan, my husband's a Patriots fan, so him and I go head-to-head every season.
But… I didn't really look like him.
Did it look like him in person?
No, I mean, the hair's a little weird.
And again, he's looked really different every year, so what were they to base it on?
Yeah, it wasn't like Dwayne Wade bad, like, that's kind of.
The all-time… the all-time worst statue, which would make me laugh every time I see it.
That is the best statue ever.
The Dwayne Wade one is also really good.
You not seen Cristiano Ronaldo before?
The Dwayne Wade one's good, too, but I thought, for all things considered, I thought statues are tough to nail.
I thought the Brady one looked pretty good.
Yeah, I wasn't… I feel like the first time I saw it, I wasn't like, wow, this is amazing, but I also wasn't like, this is horrible.
I was like, this is good.
Wow, I had a… I guess I had an unpopular opinion, then.
Yeah, no, I have some friends that are like that, Lisa.
I have some friends that are like, this is a mess.
I was like, they didn't look like it.
It's really hard to make statues.
I went to an exhibit about, like, how they built it, and, like, yeah, that's the big one.
It literally looks like a zombie apocalypse.
Here's what he looks like in real life, but it's just, like, really bad.
I still think Ronaldo's the better one, just as far as comic, like, funny.
And he's like… Top 10, like, soccer players, or…
Alright, I guess we can talk about work for a second.
I don't have too much news.
Other than that, I feel like I really want to push Maven as, like, the… on the reputation management side, I really want that to be what we're gonna do.
So I'm gonna push… we're in budget, like, now.
And we're starting to meet with teams, and I'm… I don't think it'll be the whole portfolio, because I… well, I don't… I don't know the answer yet.
I want it to be the whole portfolio.

I don't know if some of the affordable sites will pay for it, so I've been talking to some of the leadership to see if, like, corporate can pick up, or if we can do some, like, some cost sharing to see how the… how it works, but…

I really want to… to push this, because we need a tool to… we need a tool to be able to aggregate this stuff and to be able to use it, so… I really, I think you guys have the… I like the relationship stuff, like I said, I like you guys, and I like the way the tool looks, and I like the price.
So, I think that's… that's what we're gonna try to go with.
But, remains to be seen how much traction we get.
I mean, I… I'm just gonna push on people and tell them that that's what we're doing, so I'm gonna build it in there.
But, As far as the exact numbers, I don't know yet.
Trying to remember for… I think we talked about this last time.
What was the percentage of properties that were affordable versus…
A lot of them are affordable, but it's… it's… I don't know, it's more than… it's… it's probably, like.
It's a lot affordable, but that doesn't mean that only 30% of the communities would be on board.
It would… there's a lot of mixed communities, so there's a lot of, like, affordable market mix.
And then there's a lot that are, like, moderate income, so, like, those would… I think those would still really need it.
It might be, like, the low, low, just Section 8, like, Section 8 senior properties that might… like, not want to do it, but I feel like, I mean, it's pretty low… it's pretty low cost.
I think, you know, I'm optimistic.
But I gotta just beat the drum and make it happen, and get it on from a corporate perspective, so… and it might be that the management company decides to pick it up, too, so… We'll see.
So when do you typically finalize your budgets?
So they're… they're in process now, usually by October, we would… but I would know probably before then, like, I'll know sometime in September probably how things are looking, so… Okay.
I can get back to you and just kind of let you know what the… what the numbers are looking like, just for your own internal conversations about stuff, but… Yeah.
I want it to be you guys, so…
And I agree, John, like, if it's Senior Section 8, doesn't have the budget.
They don't, like, we… that's… we get it, so…
But if everything else, that makes sense.
We'd love to work with you, so I think it's beautiful.
Yeah, no, I'm optimistic here, I think.
I've been… there's been, like, so many… you guys are probably told from this, but I've had, like, a million meetings with you guys, there's… It used to be that there was, like, 3 options.

It was, like, chat meter, whatever, like, 2 other things, so it was, like… I've been kind of, like, in… I wouldn't say analysis process, but sort of like that type of thing, where there's been, like, so many things, like, Zumper has a thing, everyone's got a thing.
So… and I feel like I've owed it to everybody to talk to everyone, to do the… which has been really painful.

And then I start forgetting who has what, and I just… I keep… my main thing is that I liked what you guys have, and I keep coming back to that, so I think… that's gonna be the push, is to try to do it with you guys, and there might still be some outliers.
There's, like, a handful of properties that have the Apartments.
But… I think that's, you know, that's a whole other cost thing, so…
Yeah, I'm optimistic that we'll get… we'll get a good chunk on there.

Well, whatever you need on your end, if you need anything from us, anything that we can help with, I know I sent you the one-sheeter, and the breakdown, and the pricing, and all that good stuff, and if there's anything additional that you need in your conversations, by all means, just let us know, and we can, you know, put something together for you.
Are we doing an actual once next week, or the week after?
Yeah, I have it on for next week.
Does that still work for you, the 8th?
Patrick, you're not coming, you're not gonna be out there, are you?
I'll be here, but I'm approving expense reports.
I don't know if that means I'm paying.
Just circle the part of the bill that says, John ordered this, and then…
I spill… I spill a lot of drinks, so if there's multiple drinks on there, just don't… don't think twice.
I've never spilled a whole life… I never spilled a drink in front of Patrick in my whole life, John, I swear.
Is there somewhere good around you that you guys like to go?
Or what… you're in, wait, are you in…
Yeah, we can… do you want to just do what Old… old… the Old Faithful will?
Should we… should we go to Biltmore, or should we go somewhere else?
I like Biltmore, district is also a good option.
Do you wanna do, you wanna do district?
We could call a district, yeah.
I've never been, I'm taking a leap of faith here.
This is on you, William, because I've never seen it.
Oh yeah, Lily, I forgot about that you're there, too.
I don't go to Newton, like, ever.
So… this is gonna be a change of pace for me, I'm excited.
Like… 12, is that your, like, does it matter for you what lunch-wise time?
Yeah, we can push our team meeting.
I mean, I'm gonna drive in, so whatever.
Yeah, noon… I think noon is good.
I just shot the link in the chat.
I'm already looking at the menu, picking out, like, what I want to eat.
Oh, this looks like a vibe.
The food's not that good at the… I basically just drink at the other place, so the food's… I would have been doing us a disservice for those of you looking to actually enjoy a meal.
It's random, I know the bartender from…
Sorry, this is, like, another sidebar story, but, the bartender from the Biltmore Which, again, is just, like, an average restaurant.
is the bartender from the bar where I met my wife.
So I met my wife at the Poor House, which is now, like, a, I think it's a gay bar now.
Which is probably super fun, but it's not… not the… not the… not the place where I met my wife.

She was a… she was trained to be a waitress and bartender when I walked in there, and I had, like, the Wayne's World moment, like, where I saw her in slow motion, and I was like, I'm in love with this woman.
I actually had that… I had that happen, which is incredible.

And I spent, like, the next year and a half, because it was my favorite bar, I spent, like, the next year and a half going in there on Mondays, because I was leasing at the time, so I had Mondays off.

And, I would go in there and sit at the bar and use that as my dates, like, talking to her, because I was so afraid to ask her out and get rejected at my favorite bar, because then I wouldn't… I'm not, like, one of those guys who can get.
Yeah, you'll never be able to go back.
It's a low percentage move hitting on the bar, so, like, it's a really low percentage.
like I'm Ronaldo or something, you know, I can't just go in there and hit on the bartender.
So, it took me, like, a year to finally… and then she was going to be leaving, like, to go someplace else, and I finally asked.
I was like, well, let's go celebrate, blah blah blah, and then…

Yeah, but the bartender at the Biltmore, her dad owned the poorhouse, and she was a bartender there with my wife when I first… so I walked to Biltmore randomly, like, yeah, I hadn't seen this girl in years, and I walked in there for, like, a random lunch, and she was like, John?
And I was like, oh, hey, this is weird.
So I started going back there just because she would make me a free drink every now and then.
Restaurants, it's… Will's right, it's better to go someplace else.
I know how you met your wife.
I would have never guessed it might have been at a bar.
I always joke, like, people say drinking will get you… drinking gave me everything.
If it wasn't for my degenerate attitude, I wouldn't have my family or anything, so…
Patrick, you might want to fly in for this.
I was gonna say, I was gonna…
Yeah, I'll come out, we'll go to Alpine Valley, we'll go see some concerts out of Alpine Valley.
They have some good stuff there, I don't know, I've never been, but I was actually looking at their concert lineup recently, because I'd love to go.
It's really cool, yeah, like, a lot of the jam bands play there.
Dead and company have played a bunch.

Yeah, I went… I saw The Dead in, like, their previous… after Jerry Garcia died, they did this thing called Further Fest, where it was, like, the rest of the Grateful Dead without Jerry Garcia, and my friends and I went on tour for 45 days.

After high school, we went in, like, a minivan full, and we… Will's heard this story so many times, it's like my wife having to listen to it, but we went all the way out to Colorado and back and saw, like, all these different shows, like… so we spent 45 days camping in a tent, out of our minivan, and we sold… extra value meals.
This is terrible, but we stopped in Indiana because it was the cheapest place to buy cigarettes, and we bought cartons of cigarettes And we sold… Every night at concerts.
So at Alpine Valley, we pulled in with, like, a Coleman grill stove.
We sold what we call extra value meals.
It was a beer, bought with a fake ID.
A beer, a grilled cheese, and a pack of cigarettes was, like, $6.
And that's how we made our money to keep, like, to go… like, we bought all the tickets ahead of time with money that we made at, like, our high school jobs.
And then we survived on the road by selling extra value meals, breaking numerous federal laws.
I saw them at the Sphere twice.
Me and my buddies went out to Vegas and saw them, and I've seen, yeah, I've seen Dead and Company, like, a million times.
He didn't play with them then, though.
There's other people, but… yeah, Mayer… it took me a second, because I was like, John Mayer?
Because I'm, like, a die-hard Dead guy, but he's awesome.
Yeah, everybody I know that loves the dead loves the John… they said John Mayer, they're perfect.
Yes, it's just weird how good-looking and perfect…
When he plays, he makes the ugliest faces, but he's still magically good-looking.
Yeah, he does, he makes really funny mazes.
Alright, well, cool, we covered a lot of ground here.
I joined because I wanted to get the Bill Belichick take, because I'm genuinely curious.
I don't know any Boston sports fans, and he just got crushed last night by TCU.
I was like, how do we feel about it in Boston?
So it sounds like everybody's just Team Brady, and Belichick, take it or leave it.
People still respect him, but it's like, everyone's kind of done with it.

I also feel like the vibe here is, like, we're so desperate to get back on track that, like, the Rabel hiring, after, like, the whole Coach Mayo era, like, everyone's just looking forward to this new chapter and is putting everything else behind.
I know, it's gonna be great.
We've got a big office fantasy football draft tomorrow.
Is that tonight or tomorrow night?
Well, we're gonna see you guys on Monday, we'll see you at District, and if you need anything before then, just let us know.
